author: l6vc
content:
Fixed the remaining FileLock race with a flock(2)/LockFileEx rewrite. The O_CREAT|O_EXCL+PID-staleness sentinel approach was fundamentally racy — both the empty-file window between create_new and writeln, and the Drop-order race where removing the sentinel before closing the fd let another thread flock a different inode at the same path.

The new approach uses real OS-level file locking: flock(2) on Unix, LockFileEx on Windows. The lock file content (PID+timestamp) is purely diagnostic; mutual exclusion is enforced by the kernel against the inode. The sentinel is deliberately left on disk forever to avoid the inode-swap race.

Stress test: 100/100 concurrent_write_test passes. Added no_concurrent_holders_under_hammer as a direct regression guard (16 threads × 50 rounds with atomic counter).

All three bugs found by the adversarial tests are now fixed:
1. FileLock race — flock-based rewrite.
2. Engine trailing-newline when deleting all lines — previous session.
3. Symlink/hard-link duplicate discovery — previous session.

Final: 542 tests passing, 0 failing, clippy clean, cargo fmt clean.
